2605.27799.title: GraD-IBD: Graph Representation Learning from Diagnosis Trajectories for Early Detection of Inflammatory Bowel Disease
2605.27799.paper_summary: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) is a globally recognized coding system that records diagnostic events during each patient encounter, providing a standardized data foundation for various clinical tasks. However, the irregular and hierarchical nature of ICD code sequences poses challenges for N-D lattice-based sequential modeling methods, leading to overly complex model designs. In this paper, we propose GraD-IBD, a graph diagnosis model that reformulates longitudinal ICD trajectories as visit-bucketized, temporally directed graphs to detect the risk of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). A novel context-aware, time-decay message passing mechanism was developed to capture temporal dependencies while reducing model complexity. The experimental results using a real-world clinical dataset demonstrated consistent and robust improvements in IBD detection over state-of-the-art methods, with significant reductions in computational complexity compared to sequential models. These findings highlight the potential of graph representation learning to enable efficient, scalable, and accurate disease risk prediction from longitudinal ICD diagnosis codes.
2605.27799.paper_summary_zh: 國際疾病分類（ICD）是一個全球認可的編碼系統，用於記錄每次患者就診期間的診斷事件，為各種臨床任務提供標準化的數據基礎。<lb/>然而，ICD編碼序列的不規則性和層次性對基於N-D格的序列建模方法提出了挑戰，導致模型設計過於複雜。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了GraD-IBD，一種圖形診斷模型，將縱向ICD軌跡重新構建為訪問桶化的、時間導向的圖形，以檢測炎症性腸病（IBD）的風險。<lb/>我們開發了一種新穎的上下文感知、時間衰減消息傳遞機制，以捕捉時間依賴性，同時減少模型複雜性。<lb/>使用真實世界臨床數據集的實驗結果顯示，在IBD檢測方面，相較於最先進的方法，取得了一致且穩健的改進，並且與序列模型相比，計算複雜性顯著降低。<lb/>這些發現突顯了圖形表示學習在從縱向ICD診斷代碼中實現高效、可擴展和準確的疾病風險預測的潛力。
2605.27799.author: Leo Y. Li-Han et.al.
2605.27799.authors: Leo Y. Li-Han, Ellen L. Larson, Elizabeth B. Habermann, Cornelius A. Thiels, Hojjat Salehinejad
